channel.title: fullstackfusions
channel.description: Engineering blog — architecture, databases, and systems thinking by Mihir.

item.title: PageIndex and Vectorless RAG — A Structural Alternative for Professional Documents
item.description: Reasoning-based retrieval as an alternative to vector similarity search for structured professional documents — how PageIndex achieves 98.7% on FinanceBench, applied across healthcare, wealth management, banking, and travel with full domain use cases and implementation pathway.

item.title: Mamba and SSMs — What the Generation Backbone Change Means for RAG
item.description: A systems-level analysis of replacing the Transformer backbone with Mamba/SSM architectures in RAG systems — covering linear context scaling, constant KV cache memory, selective state tracking (Mamba-3), and the hybrid Transformer+Mamba pattern for enterprise deployment.

item.title: The RAG Supporting Stack — Memory, Prompt Engineering, Fine-tuning, and Embeddings
item.description: The cross-cutting infrastructure that makes RAG systems work in practice — layered memory architecture, prompt engineering patterns, domain-specific fine-tuning, and embedding improvements including ColBERT, cross-lingual, hyperbolic, and negation-aware approaches.

item.title: RAG in Personal Banking — Scale, AML, and Transaction Intelligence
item.description: L1–L4 RAG in personal banking — from FAQ deflection to agentic cash flow diagnosis. Covers PCI-DSS, FINTRAC/AML constraints, hybrid transaction dispute retrieval, financial health knowledge graphs, and the 10M+ daily transaction scale challenge.

item.title: RAG in Wealth Management — Fiduciary Constraints and Retrieval Design
item.description: How L1–L4 RAG applies to wealth management platforms — from product FAQs to proactive portfolio review. Covers MiFID II suitability, IPS compliance graphs, Bloomberg/Refinitiv hybrid search, and FinBERT fine-tuning.

item.title: RAG in Hospital Management Systems — Zero Hallucination Tolerance
item.description: Applying L1–L4 RAG to hospital management — clinical protocol lookup, drug interaction checking, differential diagnosis with knowledge graphs, and agentic patient surveillance. Covers HIPAA, HL7 FHIR, SNOMED CT, and clinical fine-tuning constraints.

item.title: RAG in Travel & Tourism Systems — GDS, Visa Routing, and the AI Concierge
item.description: How L1 through L4 RAG applies to travel and tourism platforms — from airline FAQ bots to agentic honeymoon planners. Covers GDS integration, visa-route graphs, multilingual embeddings, and real-time inventory constraints.

item.title: The Four RAG Levels — A Decision Framework for Enterprise Systems
item.description: L1 through L4 RAG — vanilla, hybrid, GraphRAG, and agentic — with a concrete decision framework for choosing the right retrieval level for any enterprise problem.

item.title: The Invisible Engine: What LLM Observability Actually Looks Like in Production
item.description: Classic observability was built for deterministic systems. LLMs are not deterministic. Here's the complete engineering picture — five distinct lenses, six production challenges, and the metric every AI team should be tracking but isn't.

item.title: LLM Fine-Tuning: What Nobody Tells You Until You've Burned the GPU Budget
item.description: A Principal Engineer's honest field guide to LLM fine-tuning — from LoRA and QLoRA mechanics to DPO, dataset curation, and the decision framework for when not to fine-tune.

item.title: Databases: A Lead Engineer's Field Guide to Picking the Right Tool
item.description: From OLTP to analytics, caches to graphs — how I think about database selection as a Lead Software Developer inching toward Principal.

item.title: Subdomains vs Path-Based Routing: The Architecture Decision Nobody Talks About
item.description: One routing choice quietly shapes your security posture, deployment pipeline, and scaling strategy. Here's how banks, hospitals, and big tech think about it — and how you should too.
